strategy: Round 1 he Entangles the whole team (a skipped turn) and lands a Fire Dragon DoT (100/round) — clear the DoT with Triage or Shift (Quench works if you have the first cast) and plan your setup around losing round 1.
Avoid Rank 6+ single-target attacks — they make him interrupt with Ominous Scarecrow (550) on you. Use an AoE finisher or stack blades onto a lower-rank hit.
Exploit his self-damage: attacking him makes him hit himself for ~500, and Sword of Kings wand hits always trigger it — wand him for free chip damage.
Attack with Ice or Life (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Death resist without a prism.
Stack blades and traps, then finish with an AoE or mid-rank hit to skip the Scarecrow counter.
cheatNotes: He opens by Entangling the whole team (a skipped turn) — plan your setup around losing round 1.
Round 1 he casts Fire Dragon for a long 100/round DoT (200 rounds, can crit) — clear it fast so it doesn't grind you down.
Triage or Shift safely removes that Fire Dragon DoT — bring one.
You can also Shift then Detonate the DoT back onto him as a counter-attack.
Quench dispels the Fire Dragon if you have the initial cast — an alternative to Triage.
Attacking him makes him hit himself for ~500 — free chip damage every time you swing.
Sword of Kings wand hits get reflected onto him as self-damage — wand him for extra free damage.
A Rank 6+ single-target spell makes him interrupt with Ominous Scarecrow (550) — use an AoE or lower-rank hit, or accept the 550.
